NCERT introduces section on Emergency in Class IX textbook for first time
description: For the first time, NCERT has introduced a chapter on the Emergency from 1975-77 in the Class IX curriculum. This segment sheds light on the suspension of fundamental rights and censorship, underscoring the crucial challenges faced by Indian democracy. The chapter, titled 'Understanding Society: India and Beyond,' also recognizes Jayaprakash Narayan's influence and the subsequent elections, celebrating democracy's resilience after 51 years since the Emergency was declared.

Delhi horror: After 10-year-old's rape, murder, accused cleaned cab, took ride
description: A habitual offender, Basu Singh, allegedly abducted, raped, and murdered a 10-year-old girl before attempting to meticulously erase evidence.  He changed clothes and scrubbed his cab, then resumed his usual work.  Singh, with a history of five cases including molestation and attempted murder, claimed family land disputes were behind the charges. Police apprehended him after technical surveillance.

Venezuela earthquake: State of emergency declared; death toll feared between 10,000 and 1 lakh
description: In the wake of devastating twin earthquakes, Venezuela has declared a national state of emergency. Infrastructure has been severely impacted, with many buildings reduced to rubble and fears of casualties potentially soaring between 10,000 and 100,000, according to the USGS. Rescue operations have been hindered, especially in the capital Caracas and at Maiquetia International Airport, where damage is extensive.

title: RVUNL recruitment: Short notice for 2,005 JE, JA and Accountant vacancies released
description: RVUNL Recruitment 2026: Rajasthan Rajya Vidyut Utpadan Nigam Limited (RVUNL) has announced a recruitment drive for 2,005 vacancies across five Rajasthan power companies. The openings include Junior Engineer-I, Junior Accountant and Junior Assistant/Commercial Assistant-II posts. A detailed notification and application schedule will be released in the second fortnight of July 2026. Eligible candidates aged 18 to 28 years can apply online. The selection process includes a written examination, document verification and medical examination.

title: AWS CEO says AI will 'change' jobs and explains why company is hiring 11,000 interns
description: AWS CEO Matt Garman believes AI will transform jobs, not eliminate them, citing historical examples like spreadsheet software. He argues that fears of widespread job loss are economically unsound.  Garman also highlighted the strategic importance of hiring young talent, emphasizing their adaptability, fresh perspectives, and energy, despite recent company-wide job cuts driven by AI efficiencies.

title: Is Cricket more global than Football? One has more people, the other more countries
description: As the Cricket vs Football debate resurfaces during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, this analysis argues that population figures alone don't determine a sport's global reach. While cricket's tournament nations represent more people, much of that comes from India and Pakistan. Football's strength lies in its wider geographic spread, while cricket enjoys deeper popularity in a smaller number of densely populated countries.

Traders are now anticipating a surplus as physical crude trades at discounts.

description: Eighteen-year-old Anushka Yadav etched her name in history at the National Inter-State Championships, becoming the youngest Indian athletics national record holder. She shattered the women's hammer throw record with a remarkable 67.02m, surpassing the previous mark twice during the competition. Hailing from a farming family, Yadav's journey from rural Uttar Pradesh to national glory is an inspiring tale of dedication and talent.

Trump dismissed own govt’s data, slapped tariffs on India
description: A new book reveals President Trump's disregard for factual trade data concerning India, often dismissing official figures as 'bullshit numbers' to justify his imposition of high tariffs.  Despite evidence from USTR and trade representatives, Trump insisted on inflated tariff rates, leading to strained US-India relations.  This approach extended to foreign policy, where his administration prioritized 'feelings over facts,' impacting global partnerships.

Hokit, however, continues to stand by his words and has refused to apologize.

description: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son, 68, plans to lead the tech giant for another decade, focusing intensely on artificial intelligence and robotics. He aims to achieve 'artificial superintelligence' and is backing this vision with significant investments in companies like Arm and OpenAI, alongside robotics acquisitions.

Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo face off in football’s net worth race
description: Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have spent nearly two decades battling for football supremacy. This time, the contest is about money. Both legends have crossed the billionaire mark and stand among the richest athletes in the world. Their fortunes have surged through football contracts, sponsorships, business ventures, and social media influence. While Ronaldo is believed to hold a slight edge in total wealth, Messi is not far behind in this remarkable financial race.

title: Nirjala Ekadashi: Date, parana time, puja vidhi, mantra and significance
description: Hindus observe Nirjala Ekadashi on June 25, 2026, a highly auspicious day dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This strict fast, observed without food or water until the next day, is believed to bestow the benefits of all 24 Ekadashis and cleanse past sins. Devotees engage in prayers, donations, and chanting, with the fast breaking on Dwadashi after specific rituals. Health precautions are advised due to the summer observance.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM swaps 1,000 workers with 50 robots
description: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.
